name: pide
ide_drive: single -drive if=ide,format=raw,index=0,file=pide.img (primary master, unit 0); generated by gen_disk.py as the single source of truth for BOTH the qemu backing image (pide.img) and the RTL backing store (pide.disk.hex via $readmemh)
transitions: real->protected (CR0.PE 0->1, CS far-jump to 32-bit flat code segment 0x08)
set nIEN (0x3F6 bit1) -> polled mode, NO IRQ14 ever delivered (fully synchronous, per-record single-step differentiable)

select primary master (0x1F6=0xA0); read RESET SIGNATURE: status(0x1F7)=0x50, error(0x1F1)=0x00, nsector(0x1F2)=1, sector(0x1F3)=1, lcyl(0x1F4)=0, hcyl(0x1F5)=0, devhead(0x1F6)=0xA0, alt-status(0x3F6)=0x50

absent slave: select unit1 (0x1F6=0xB0); status(0x1F7)/error(0x1F1)/alt-status(0x3F6) read 0x00 (qemu masks ONLY these three for the selected absent slave); the slave's INDEPENDENT register shadow returns lcyl/hcyl=0xFF (fresh signature, read EARLY before any LBA write broadcasts over it) — M8.4e; reselect master

M8.4e2 absent-slave INDEPENDENT nsector/sector (per-IDEState, not shared): after a master READ LBA0 advances the MASTER to nsector=0/sector=1, selecting the absent slave (0xB0) reads its own last-broadcast nsector=1/sector=0 (qemu advances only the selected drive's regs) — GATE-PROVES the shadow is independent of the master's command advance, not a vacuous shared read

M8.4e2 idle/out-of-window data-port read: an 0x1F0 read with DRQ clear returns 0x0000 (qemu ide_data_readw returns 0 unless DRQ && pio-out), tightening the prior stale-buffer-word boundary; also eliminates any X-leak on the media-less CD

IDENTIFY DEVICE (0xEC): poll BSY=0/DRQ=1 (status 0x58), drain 256 words via inw (each graded per-record): geometry w0=0x0040/w1=cyls=2/w3=heads=16/w6=secs=63, capacity/feature words, config-pinned model 'QEMU HARDDISK'/serial 'QM00001'/firmware '2.5+'; status after drain = 0x50
READ SECTORS (0x20) LBA 0: nsector=1, LBA28 devhead 0xE0; drain 256 words byte-identical to the single-source disk (word 255 = 0xAA55 boot sig); status 0x50
READ SECTORS (0x20) LBA 127 (non-adjacent): proves LBA->offset addressing (sector-127 word 0 = 0x7F00); status 0x50

READ SECTORS (0x20) nsector=2 @ LBA 0 (MULTI-SECTOR): drains 512 words across the sector-0->sector-1 boundary, GATE-PROVING the multi-sector PIO continuation (data_idx==255 && nsec_left>1 -> xfer_lba+1, reload DRQ) matches qemu (req_nb_sectors=1, per-sector DRQ window re-armed by ide_sector_read_cb); status 0x50 after both

WRITE SECTORS (0x30) single @ scratch LBA 64 (M8.4b): status 0x58 immediately after 0x30 (DRQ-out, no BSY), write 256 words via outw (pattern 0xC000+j, distinct from native 0x4000+j), status 0x50 after commit; read-back LBA 64 returns 0xC0xx per-record — PROVES the write took effect (non-vacuous)

WRITE SECTORS (0x30) multi @ LBA 70 nsector=2 (M8.4b): status 0x58 immediate, write sector 0 (256 words), status 0x58 at the boundary (DRQ RE-ARMED — the load-bearing multi-sector-write record), write sector 1 (256 words), status 0x50 after both; read-back LBA 70-71 (512 words) returns the written 0xC000..0xC1FF per-record — GATE-PROVES the WRITE continuation FSM + the data of both sectors
EXECUTE DEVICE DIAGNOSTIC (0x90): error(0x1F1)=0x01 (device 0 passed), status=0x50, devhead=0xA0 (ATA_DEV_ALWAYS_ON)

M8.4c PIO FIDELITY (10 directed sequences, all gate-proven non-vacuously): OOR single READ @128 (upfront abort 0x41/0x04, no DRQ, sector reg 0x80 unchanged) + follow-up READ LBA0 (disk untouched: w0=0x0000/w255=0xAA55) + LBA-register advance (after the in-range nsec=1 read: nsector=0, sector=1, select=0xE0); OOR single WRITE @129 (arms 0x58, fill 256 dropped, abort 0x41/0x04 on the last word, sector reg 0x81 not advanced) + alias LBA1 read (w0=0x0100 native, NOT corrupted); multi-READ crossing @127 nsec=2 (sector 127 delivered real data, abort 0x41/0x04 at the boundary, sector reg 0x80 advanced to the OOR LBA); multi-WRITE crossing @127 nsec=2 (sector 127 committed 0xB000+j + re-arm 0x58, sector 128 OOR dropped + abort 0x41 on its last word) + LBA127 read-back (0xB000+j proves commit-before-abort); CHS C0/H1/S2 -> LBA64 (data w0=0x4000, status 0x50); command-while-DRQ (a mid-DRQ 0xEC is DROPPED: status stays 0x58, error 0x00, the original READ-LBA0 data intact w0=0x0000 not IDENTIFY 0x0040); read-during-write (a stray inw in a write window returns 0x0000 + consumes no slot, the 256-word fill stays aligned, read-back LBA100 = 0xD0xx); misc 0x70 SEEK / 0x10 RECAL / 0x91 INIT-DEV-PARAMS (each status 0x50, error 0x00). Plus the per-command error-register clear (qemu core.c:2168) so a prior abort's 0x04 never lingers.

M8.4d ATA command-set: SET MULTIPLE (0xC6) accept pow2<=16 -> 0x50 / abort 0x41 (3,32) — does NOT patch the cached IDENTIFY w59 (stays 0x0110, qemu cmd_set_multiple_mode is runtime-only); SET FEATURES (0xEF) 0x03 udma2 patches cached w88 0x203F->0x043F, 0x02 wcache-enable w85=0x4021, 0x82 wcache-DISABLE completes (NOT abort) w85=0x4001, 0x99 unsupported -> abort; the re-IDENTIFY grades w59=0x0110/w88=0x043F/w85=0x4001; SRST (0x3F6 bit2 edge) synchronous signature restore (dirty 0x41 -> 0x50/error 0x01/signature, no BSY)

M8.4e SECONDARY CHANNEL (empty ATAPI CD-ROM master, qemu's auto-created ide1-cd0): decoded at 0x170-0x177 + control 0x376 (nIEN set first). SEC-1 signature 0xEB14 (lcyl=0x14/hcyl=0xEB marks ATAPI vs the HD's 0x0000), status 0x50; SEC-2 EXECUTE DIAGNOSTIC (0x90) -> status 0x00 (ATAPI clears READY_STAT, NOT 0x50), error 0x01, signature; SEC-3 IDENTIFY DEVICE (0xEC) -> ABORT 0x41/0x04 + signature re-set (cmd_identify CD branch: ide_set_signature then ide_abort_command); SEC-4 READ SECTORS (0x20) -> ABORT 0x41 (no DRQ; an HD gives 0x58); SEC-5 secondary absent SLAVE masked status/error + independent lcyl/hcyl=0xFF

M8.4e2 ATAPI command-specific surface (review fold-back, all gate-proven non-vacuously, byte-identical to qemu): DEVICE RESET (0x08, CD_OK) -> ide_reset+signature, status 0x00, error 0x00, nsector/sector=1, lcyl/hcyl=0x14/0xEB, devhead 0xA0 (head cleared), NO IRQ (cmd_device_reset returns false); SET FEATURES (0xEF, ALL_OK) -> COMPLETES status 0x50/error 0x00 (the empty CD still has a BlockBackend); FLUSH CACHE (0xE7, ALL_OK by permission) -> ABORTS 0x41/0x04 at RUNTIME on the empty CD (blk_aio_flush no-medium error via ide_flush_cb/ide_handle_rw_error — OBSERVED in the golden, NOT the 0x50 a populated drive gives); unsupported opcode bare abort (0xB0 SMART = HD_CFA_OK, NOT CD_OK -> ide_abort_command WITHOUT ide_set_signature): a lcyl pre-written to 0x55 SURVIVES (task file untouched), status 0x41/error 0x04; SRST on the secondary (0x376 bit2) -> ATAPI post-reset status 0x00 (not 0x50), error 0x01, signature restored

M8.4f-pre PCI config (PIIX3 IDE function 00:01.1, via a minimal single-function ventium_soc config shim at 0xCF8/0xCFC): the FIRST exercise of the 32-bit outl/inl (io_size=4) I/O path under soc_en (verified to retire byte-identical — only the config DATA differed pre-shim). Reads vendor/device reg0x00=0x70108086, class/prog-if reg0x08=0x01018000, PCI_COMMAND reg0x04 pre-enable=0x0000; programs BAR4 reg0x20=0xC000 -> reads back 0x0000C001 (low 4 bits read-only: bit0=1 I/O indicator, 16-byte region) — vs the unmapped 0x00000001 before; enables PCI_COMMAND IO(bit0)+bus-master(bit2)=0x0005. This MAPS the bus-master IDE BAR4 the M8.4f DMA engine will decode (empirically: BMIDE reads 0xFF until PCI_COMMAND.IO is set, so a fixed-port hard-decode is NOT differentiable). All graded per-record EQUIVALENT vs qemu-system-i386 8.2.2
deterministic compute (esi=cyls=2, edi=0x7F017F00 sector-127 words 0/1, ebp=0x7F00AA55 sector-0 boot-sig low half, ecx=diag-error=1), isa-debug-exit code 133
why_per_record_differential: pide sets nIEN FIRST on BOTH channels so NO IRQ14/15 is ever delivered — every IDE interaction is a synchronous IN/OUT or a polled-status read — so qemu's gdbstub single-step (gen_trace.py --system) IS a valid per-record oracle (the SSTEP_NOIRQ HW-IRQ masking is structurally irrelevant: no IRQ is raised). The gate is plain compare.py EQUIVALENT (22947 records), running on ventium_soc with the M8.4 IDE device (primary ATA HD master + secondary empty ATAPI CD master) + the -drive image.
disk_value_match: gen_disk.py emits BOTH artifacts from ONE in-memory byte buffer: pide.img (qemu -drive if=ide,format=raw,index=0) and pide.disk.hex (ven_ide $readmemh). They cannot drift (a build-time drift assert reconstructs the buffer and compares). qemu's ide_data_readw is cpu_to_le16 (identity on the LE host) so a 16-bit data-port read = (buf[2j+1]<<8)|buf[2j]; the RTL returns {disk[2j+1],disk[2j]} for the same j -> byte-identical READ SECTORS by construction. Disk geometry 128 sectors -> qemu guess_chs_for_size = cyls 2/heads 16/secs 63 (ven_ide parameterized identically). NOTE: CYLS is an INDEPENDENT ven_ide parameter (not derived from DISK_SECTORS); the geometry coupling is a CONVENTION pinned to the committed 128/2/16/63 config (a code comment, not an enforced assert) — changing DISK_SECTORS without re-deriving CYLS/HEADS/SECS would diverge the IDENTIFY geometry words, which the gate would then catch.
identify_pinning: ven_ide builds the IDENTIFY 256-word block: the geometry words (w0/1/3/4/5/6/54/55/56/57/58/60/61/100) are COMPUTED from the DISK_SECTORS/CYLS/HEADS/SECS parameters; the model/serial/firmware strings (w10-46) and the feature/capability words (w20-22/47-53/59/62-69/80-88/93/106/169) are config-pinned constants captured from qemu 8.2.2 for the fixed single -drive if=ide,index=0 config (e.g. w85 WCE = 0x4021, the OBSERVED value, NOT a spec guess). These are documented build-pinned data (same category as the disk image); the per-record compare is REAL — the committed RTL constants are graded against a FRESHLY generated golden each run, so any qemu drift is caught.
oracle_boundaries: OFF the differential surface, documented (all on paths the directed test provably does NOT reach, so the EQUIVALENT result is honest): (1) IRQ14 line-edge — nIEN-set so no IRQ is ever raised; the line is wired to PIC IR14 (quiescent, like IR1/IR8/IR12). (2) inter-instruction async-BSY-vs-synchronous timing — qemu's block READ + WRITE-completion write-back settle in an async BH BEFORE the next single-step (golden never shows a lingering BSY/0xD0); the RTL settles synchronously -> same poll count (21875 records match). (a) ABSENT SLAVE — NOW MODELED + GATE-PROVEN (M8.4e/e2): qemu masks only error/status/alt-status to 0x00 for the selected absent slave (the test reads those, matching) and returns the slave's INDEPENDENT register shadow for the rest; this RTL models lcyl/hcyl=0xFF AND nsector/sector (reset 1, broadcast-tracked, NOT advanced by a master command), gate-proven by reading the slave nsector(=1)/sector(=0) after a master READ advanced the MASTER to 0/1. Residual: the slave error/status HOB paths are unmodeled (only the masked 0 is read). (b) data-port read OUTSIDE a read-DRQ window — NOW MODELED (M8.4e2): returns 0x0000 (qemu ide_data_readw returns 0 unless DRQ && pio-out) for idle/write-window/empty-CD reads alike, gate-proven by an idle 0x1F0 read. (c) the SECONDARY channel (0x170-0x177/0x376) — NOW DECODED + GATE-PROVEN (M8.4e): instantiated as the empty ATAPI CD-ROM master (IS_ATAPI=1/HAS_DISK=0), per-record EQUIVALENT to qemu's auto-created ide1-cd0. (d) the WRITE word-by-word disk[] commit (RTL per-word vs qemu's end-of-sector buffer commit) — identical CPU-observable result (the LBA is not re-read until a later READ). NOW MODELED + GATE-PROVEN in M8.4c (no longer boundaries): the OOR-LBA clean abort (READ upfront / WRITE + multi-crossing deferred-after-fill), CHS addressing, the command-while-DRQ ignore, the read-during-write 0x0000 guard, the misc 0x70/0x10/0x91 completions, the per-command error-register clear, AND the LBA-register advance FOR single-sector transfers + the FINAL-sector value of a multi-sector transfer (R_RA_*, R_OORR_SEC, R_MRC_SEC). ALSO MODELED but not separately gate-tested: the mid-DRQ task-file-register WRITE drop (ven_ide gates 0x1F1-0x1F6 writes on !r_status[3], matching qemu core.c:1287) — a clean gate test would also sample the register-advance LAG (next), so it is value-verified out-of-band like the command-abort. REMAINING boundaries surfaced by the M8.4c review (all gate-unreachable): (e) MISC-COMMAND STATE SIDE-EFFECTS — 0x70/0x10/0x91 return the correct 0x50/0x00 completion but their seek/geometry state is ignored; cmd_specify (0x91) mutates qemu's current heads/sectors which would shift IDENTIFY words 55/56 AND drive CHS address translation (ide_get_sector core.c:631) on a SUBSEQUENT op; the test never re-IDENTIFYs or does a post-0x91 CHS transfer. (f) LBA-MODE MULTI-SECTOR REGISTER TRAJECTORY: (i) nsector (0x1F2) is forced to 0 at the first sector boundary instead of qemu's per-sector decrement (and stays 0 on a crossing-abort vs qemu's remaining count); (ii) the READ visible LBA registers LAG qemu by one sector DURING the transfer (the RTL advances at end-of-drain; qemu advances at DRQ-window-open in ide_sector_read_cb). Both off-surface: the test reads the task-file regs only POST-transfer (where they agree: R_RA_SEC=1) or at the crossing-abort (where both land on the OOR LBA: R_MRC_SEC=0x80), never mid-transfer. (g) CHS-mode REGISTER ADVANCE off-by-one — after a CHS transfer qemu writes the 1-based CHS sector (ide_set_sector CHS branch core.c:657) whereas the RTL writes the LBA28 decomposition; the test reads no task-file reg after a CHS transfer. M8.4d landed SET MULTIPLE (0xC6), SET FEATURES (0xEF incl 0x82), and SRST (0x3F6 bit2). Deferred to M8.4d2/later: the 0x91 INIT-DEV-PARAMS geometry side-effect (e) + the CHS-mode register-advance (g) (Tier 2 — add combinational divides, legacy-niche), the LBA-mode multi-sector register-trajectory residuals (f), READ/WRITE MULTIPLE (0xC4/0xC5, the N-sector DRQ window), LBA48 (0x24/0x34), the SET FEATURES reset_reverts side-effect (0xCC/0x66 complete 0x50 but their SRST-revert flag is unmodeled — unobservable without 0x91 geometry), other non-{EC,20,21,30,31,70,10,91,90,C6,EF} ATA-HD commands, and bus-master DMA. M8.4e landed the SECONDARY channel + the empty ATAPI CD-ROM (signature/DIAGNOSTIC/IDENTIFY-abort) and M8.4e2 its command-specific surface (0x08/0xE7/0xEF + bare aborts + slave nsector/sector + idle data-port). The one ACKNOWLEDGED ATAPI DIVERGENCE: 0xA0 PACKET (cmd_packet) and 0xA1 IDENTIFY-PACKET (cmd_identify_packet) are CD_OK and qemu DRQ-enters them (status 0x58), whereas this RTL bare-aborts (0x41/0x04) — the deferred full-ATAPI-PACKET milestone; the directed test issues NEITHER, so the EQUIVALENT is honest.
comparison_plan: verif/soc/run-soc-ide-gate.sh: (0) gen_disk.py emits pide.img + pide.disk.hex + drift assert; (1) build pide.bin + the ventium_soc --soc TB with -DVEN_IDE_DISK_HEX; (2) confirm pide.bin reaches isa-debug-exit (133) under qemu-system-i386 WITH the -drive (drive MANDATORY: no-drive aborts IDENTIFY/READ); (3) regenerate the per-record golden via gen_trace.py --system --args -drive ... (records drift-checked vs the committed pide.sys.vtrace.golden); (4) run ventium_soc on pide.bin; (5) compare.py --mode func -> EQUIVALENT over all retired instructions.
description: Bare-metal M8.4a/b SoC IDE/ATA differential (NO paging, NO interrupts — nIEN-polled). Boots real->protected, then synchronously exercises the IDE/ATA controller M8.4 wires into ventium_soc — the primary channel command block (0x1F0-0x1F7) + control block (0x3F6), one drive (primary master, PIO mode): reset signature, absent-slave masking, IDENTIFY DEVICE, READ SECTORS (LBA 0 + 127, single + a 2-sector multi-sector read), WRITE SECTORS (M8.4b: single @ scratch LBA 64 + multi-sector @ LBA 70/71, each proved by a read-back of the written pattern), EXECUTE DEVICE DIAGNOSTIC. Every interaction is a synchronous IN/OUT or polled read, graded PER-RECORD against the qemu-system-i386 single-step golden (with a matching -drive). The disk is a single-source-of-truth image (gen_disk.py -> pide.img for qemu + pide.disk.hex for the RTL $readmemh), so READ SECTORS (and the WRITE read-back) data is byte-identical by construction; qemu writes go to a snapshot=on overlay so the committed pide.img stays pristine (an md5 PRE==POST gate assert enforces it). M8.4e adds the SECONDARY channel as an empty ATAPI CD-ROM master (0xEB14 signature, DIAGNOSTIC status 0x00, IDENTIFY/READ abort) + the absent-slave independent register shadow; M8.4e2 (review fold-back) the command-specific ATAPI surface (DEVICE RESET 0x08, SET FEATURES 0xEF complete, FLUSH CACHE 0xE7 runtime-abort, bare aborts leaving the task file untouched, ATAPI SRST status 0x00), the slave's independent nsector/sector, and the idle/out-of-window data-port-read 0. Proves the M8.4 IDE wiring byte-identical to qemu over all 22947 retired instructions; the IRQ14/15 cadence + async-BSY timing + the deferred full-ATAPI-PACKET (0xA0/0xA1) are documented oracle boundaries off the differential surface.
